Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver To Aid In Execution Of Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-03-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motin And Motion To Compel Further Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: ASSIGNEE DAVID M. MCKIM's Motion To Compel Further Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories is GRANTED. Since McKim failed to seek sanctions, failed to signify a request for sanctions in caption, and failed to offer proof in declaration of time spent, rates charged, or costs incurred, sanctions as against all Parties are DENIED. Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association, Inc.'s ("CTA") submits a Limited Opposition to Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Post-Judgment Interrogatory, in which it agrees to comply, in part, with Interrogatory No. 1, with the exception that it "continues to object to the defendant's request to provide the attorney of record, if any, for each defendant and the prayer of the complaint." Notably, CTA fails to submit responses in a Separate Statement, which can be fatal, in and of itself. See Cal. R. Ct. Rule 3.1345; Mills v. U.S. Bank, 166 Cal. App. 4th 871, 893 (2008). In Reply to CTA's partial capitulation, McKim seems satisfied, "request[ing] a thirty day postponement" of the instant Motion "to see if [CTA] indeed follows through on its 'agreement.'" ) But McKim then contradicts himself, stating in the next sentence that it is not satisfied with CTA's response, calling it "bad faith gamesmanship" and then in the last sentence of the Reply demands that CTA respond to Interrogatory 1 in "full" and "without objection." CTA briefly complains in its "Limited Opposition" that it should not be compelled to provide the information requested insofar as "this would require hours of clerical or professional time to review each file." But CTA fails to offer a declaration in support of this contention, and does not quantify whether "hours" of time means two hours or twenty hours. In the absence of facts submitted in declaration with more specificity, the Court cannot presume and does not know that responding to Interrogatory 1 would be unduly burdensome. (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312.) Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
